23.display: 2x + 3 has no equals sign, so it cannot be solved to find a specific value of x — it is an expression, not an equation.

24.display: Never — an expression has no equals sign, so it can only be evaluated for a given x, not solved for a single value.

25.display: The ≡ symbol means "is identically equal to" — the two sides are equal for every possible value of x, not just one specific value as with =.

26.display: Expression: no equals sign, e.g. 3x + 2. Equation: has an equals sign and can be solved for a value, e.g. 3x + 2 = 11. Formula: relates two or more different quantities, e.g. P = 4s.

27.display: True — x + x + x = 3x for every value of x, so it holds as an identity, not just for one solution.

28.display: P = 4s is a formula because it relates two different quantities (perimeter and side length) so you can use it to calculate one from the other, rather than solving for a single unknown value.

29.display: From 5x + 2 = 17 you can solve to find the specific value x = 3; from the expression 5x + 2 alone you can only evaluate it for a given x, not find x itself.

30.display: 2x + 3 has no equals sign at all, so Mo's reasoning is wrong from the start — an equation must contain an equals sign, and 2x + 3 does not.

38.display: 3(x + 2) + 1 = 3x + 6 + 1 = 3x + 7, which matches, so the expressions are equivalent.

40.display: Example: 2x + 3 has no equals sign (expression). 2x + 3 = 9 has an equals sign and one solution, x = 3 (equation). 2(x + 3) ≡ 2x + 6 is true for every value of x (identity).
